description_long: This is a terracotta vase with a rounded body, a single curved handle, and a slightly flared spout. The surface is adorned with black-figure painted motifs, including a human figure in motion and stylized floral elements, reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery. The handle and rim show signs of darkened wear, possibly from age or use. The painted decoration is partially faded, especially on one side, but the main figures and patterns remain discernible. The vase's surface is matte with a slightly rough texture, typical of hand-crafted ceramics. Its size and form suggest it was designed for holding liquids or as a decorative piece.
description: A terracotta vase with black-figure painted motifs, a single handle, and a flared spout, showing signs of age and wear. Decorative human and floral patterns adorn its rounded body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main painted human figure and a large floral motif, with the handle visible to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the handle, with only partial floral motifs visible on either side.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the handle and a faded painted figure, with floral decoration and visible wear on the surface.
description_view_3: View from the opposite side of the spout, showing both painted figures and the dotted pattern connecting them, with the handle in the background.
attribute_pattern: figural and floral motifs with dotted lines
